title: Quiz: Trainability Theory
description: Test your understanding of the theoretical foundations of trainability

questions.question: What is the Dynamical Lie Algebra (DLA) of a quantum circuit?

options.text: The set of all possible output states
The algebra generated by gate generators and their commutators
The classical algebra of circuit parameters

questions.explanation: The DLA is the smallest Lie algebra containing the gate generators, formed by taking all nested commutators. Its dimension determines circuit expressibility.

questions.question: What does it mean for a circuit ensemble to form a 2-design?

options.text: Its first two statistical moments match Haar-random unitaries

questions.explanation: A 2-design is an ensemble whose first and second moments (averages of U⊗U* terms) match those of Haar-random unitaries.

questions.question: According to Levy's Lemma, how do Lipschitz functions concentrate on high-dimensional spheres?
questions.explanation: Levy's Lemma shows that Lipschitz functions on high-dimensional spheres concentrate exponentially around their mean, with probability of deviation decaying as exp(-cd).

questions.question: A circuit with polynomial DLA dimension necessarily avoids barren plateaus.
questions.explanation: While polynomial DLA dimension is necessary for avoiding expressibility-induced barren plateaus, other factors like cost function locality and initialization also matter.

questions.question: For a global cost function with a circuit forming a 2-design, how does gradient variance scale?
questions.explanation: For 2-design circuits with global cost, Var[∂C/∂θ] ~ O(1/d³) = O(2^{-3n}), showing severe exponential suppression.

questions.question: What is the dimension of su(d) for d-dimensional Hilbert space?
questions.explanation: The special unitary algebra su(d) has dimension d² - 1 (traceless Hermitian matrices). This is the maximum DLA dimension.

questions.question: How does the variance bound change for a k-local cost function?

options.text: It becomes O(2^k/d), exponentially better for small k
It stays the same as global

questions.explanation: For k-local cost functions, the variance scales as O(2^k/d), which is exponentially better than global (O(1/d³)) when k is small.

questions.question: Which conditions together can guarantee trainability? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Polynomial DLA (a), local costs (b), and identity initialization (c) together help ensure trainability. Maximum depth (d) would worsen barren plateaus.
